codeReview.summary: The feature wires a home-selection and accessory-discovery flow, but the implementation does not preserve the active-home choice in the UI and its new tests are largely non-executable placeholders that fail to validate the required behavior.

issues.description: When exactly one home exists, `loadHomes()` auto-selects it and immediately navigates away without ever updating `homes` to mark that choice active, so the user never sees the required visible active-home selection before transition. This violates the feature requirement that the active home choice be shown instead of silently selected.

The added HomeSelectionService tests do not exercise the real feature behavior: `MockHomeKitAdapter2.fetchHomes()` always returns an empty array and `authorizationStatus` always returns authorized regardless of the actor state, so tests like `testAvailableHomes_ReturnsAllHomesWithActiveStatus` and `testActiveHome_ReturnsErrorWhenPermissionDenied` cannot meaningfully validate home enumeration, persistence, or permission handling. The transcript also shows repeated build/test failures while trying to make these tests compile, so the claimed coverage is unreliable.

The accessory discovery tests are also placeholders rather than end-to-end checks of the service: comments explicitly state the core `discoverAccessories(in:)` path cannot be tested, and the mock adapter returns no HMHome/HMAccessory values. As a result, the suite does not verify room grouping, filtering, or stale-result clearing for actual discovery results, leaving the main expected behavior unproven.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The apple-integration-worker skill requires reading `.factory/library/automation-strategy.md`, `.factory/library/environment.md`, and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, writing failing tests first, and returning to the orchestrator if required real-device validation is unavailable. The transcript skeleton shows this worker skipped those library reads, implemented code before stable tests existed, and still handed off success without any real-device validation even though the skill says to return rather than guess when that proof is unavailable.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apple-integration-worker/SKILL.md procedure steps 1, 4, and 8-9 versus worker transcript skeleton for session 45167f9f-967a-4213-8d29-666e2e844125, which only reads mission/AGENTS/architecture, adds tests after implementation/debugging, and reports no interactive checks.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, commit diff, code, skill file, AGENTS/services/architecture context, and transcript skeleton. Result: fail due to a missed UX requirement around visible active-home selection and because the newly added tests do not actually validate the implemented home-selection/accessory-discovery behavior.
